measurement_note: Size is n, the binary-string length. Every tier is a legal all-zero string, so all input characters affect the forced survivor position and the expected result is n-1 ones followed by zero. The Accepted first-index/count construction and an independent one-pass zero scan take O(n) time. A correct implementation that recomputes the global zero statistics for every output position takes O(n^2), completes all 8/32/128-character tiers, and is rejected only by scaling.
